notes: The change is a lint message string update in src/messages.ts, updating 'Avoid dynamic code execution.' to 'Avoid eval(code) and new Function(code) — both execute arbitrary strings.' The word 'eval' appears only in a string literal within a comment/message context, not as a dynamic code execution.
Diff Drift correctly reports 0 active flags for this change — the mention of eval() in a string literal is not itself a security risk.
The change improves the clarity of the lint rule message and poses no security risk to the codebase.
